summary: Iteration 4 backend + frontend validated. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests passing — kiosk public register (200 / 400 dup IC / 400 dup email), register→checkin queue assignment with auto-doctor, PATCH /appointments/{id} scheduled_at + doctor_id + reason all work and broadcast, POST /appointments/block enforces doctor role (admin/patient/pharmacist all 403), regression flows (kiosk lookup, pharmacy queue, availability slots, doctors list, 4-role login) intact. Frontend: Kiosk register flow E2E works (unknown IC → kiosk-register-screen → fills name → submits → PrintChit queue #032 with auto-picked Dr. Wei Tan); known IC IC-880421-14-5567 correctly skips register and shows kiosk-found-screen. DoctorScheduler renders, sched-prev/next navigate days, + block creates a grey 'Blocked' slot with sched-unblock-<id>, native HTML5 drag-and-drop (manually dispatched) successfully moves an appointment to a new slot and toast confirms 'Moved #12 → 09:30'. Two issues found.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking the X (sched-unblock-<id>) on a Blocked slot calls PATCH /appointments/{id} {status: 'cancelled'} — but the slot is already status=cancelled (that's how blocks are stored). The PATCH succeeds with no-op and load() returns the same block, so the grey 'Blocked' chip remains in the UI. Either (a) load() must filter out is_block && status==='cancelled' after unblock by tracking a client-side 'removed' set, (b) use a different status like 'unblocked'/'archived' for removed blocks, or (c) issue a DELETE endpoint for blocks. Without a fix, doctors cannot remove a block from the day planner via the UI.

Accessibility warnings: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle' and missing aria-describedby on the PrintChit modal. Screen readers will not announce it correctly. Recommend wrapping the title in <DialogTitle> (or VisuallyHidden) and providing <DialogDescription>.

action_items: FIX: DoctorScheduler.unblock — current PATCH status='cancelled' is a no-op for blocks (they're already cancelled). Decide on a removal mechanism: e.g., (1) add DELETE /api/appointments/{id} for is_block=true rows, (2) PATCH is_block=false + status='cancelled' so load() filters them out, or (3) introduce a 'removed' flag. Update DoctorScheduler.jsx 'unblock' + load() filter accordingly.
FIX (a11y): Add <DialogTitle> and <DialogDescription> (or VisuallyHidden) to PrintChit dialog to clear Radix DialogContent accessibility warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1431 lines — exceeds the 700-line threshold. Consider splitting into routers (auth, kiosk, appointments, pharmacy, ai, availability, files) for maintainability.

block_time endpoint stores blocks as status='cancelled' which conflates real cancellations with intentional blocks. Querying historical 'cancelled' appointments will mix the two. A dedicated status='blocked' (and corresponding queue exclusion) is cleaner and avoids the unblock bug above.
DoctorScheduler.load() does not filter by status, so cancelled real appointments (not just blocks) will also render on the planner. Filter (x.is_block || x.status !== 'cancelled') for correctness.
kiosk_register endpoint is PUBLIC (no auth). Recommend basic rate-limiting / captcha later to prevent spam patient creation.
test_credentials: dr.tan@medilink.io / Doctor@123, admin@medilink.io / Admin@123, pharmacy@medilink.io / Pharm@123, patient1@medilink.io / Patient@123 (IC IC-880421-14-5567). Fresh kiosk-register IC pattern: IC-TEST-<uuid>
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed called once at session start. Test data prefixed TEST_ — TEST_Walkin Bob, TEST_Flow Walker, TEST_Playwright Walker (kiosk-registered patients) and TEST_iter4_patch / TEST_blk_admin / TEST_lunch_break / TEST_to_unblock appointments + blocks remain in DB and should be purged by main agent if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py and are stable (uses uuid-based unique ICs per run). Drag-and-drop in headless Playwright requires manually dispatching DragEvent with a shared DataTransfer instance — see /app/test_reports/iteration_4.json action #2 in screenshots. The unblock UI bug is reproducible: log in as dr.tan, click any + block on the day planner, then click the X — block remains visible.
rca of the issue: Unblock bug RCA: blocks are created with is_block=true & status='cancelled' (server.py block_time endpoint). DoctorScheduler.unblock() then PATCHes status='cancelled' on the same row — backend updates nothing meaningful, broadcasts an event, then frontend reload() pulls the same list (no status filter), so the Blocked chip persists. Repro: doctor login → DoctorScheduler → '+' on empty slot → 'X' on the resulting Blocked chip → chip stays. Mitigation: either filter (a.is_block && a.status==='cancelled') with a client-side hidden set OR add DELETE /api/appointments/{id} OR change unblock to PATCH is_block=false.
